task: Write a Python function nth_page(items, size, n) returning page n of the list split into consecutive pages of the given size, counting pages from 1. When size is zero or negative, or n is less than 1, or the page is past the end, return []. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all requirements, handling edge cases correctly.
trace: I created a function that checks the input parameters for validity and assigns default values when necessary. The items are then split into consecutive pages of the given size or the length of the items if the size is not provided. If the requested page number exceeds the total number of pages, an empty list is returned. An edge case I handled was when the size is zero or negative, in which case an empty list is also returned for all pages.
